title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Common Term Insurance FAQs answered
Meta Description: Despite the fact that term insurance is the most economical kind of life insurance, many customers have concerns regarding its features and advantages.
Keywords: term insurance faqs, term life insurance questions, term insurance, term plan premium calculator, term insurance benefits
________________________________________________________
Introduction
A term insurance plan is one that provides guaranteed financial protection for your loved ones. Having a term insurance plan is one of the most fundamental aspects that leads to a successful and effective financial corpus for anybody these days. This is because a regular term insurance plan may be used to provide financial stability in the future for your family. When it comes to selecting a term insurance policy, it's essential to consider a few aspects. These are the elements that determine the term insurance benefits you will get after purchasing the plan.
As a result, it is essential that you have a thorough grasp of the terms and conditions of the term plans offered by different insurance providers so that you can make an educated choice when selecting a term insurance plan that matches your individual needs and expectations. The following is a collection of the most common term life insurance questions:
Common Term Insurance FAQs
Q: How important are personal behaviors such as smoking when picking a term insurance plan?
Premium terms differ between smokers and non-smokers, and premium terms are normally higher for smokers since they fall into the high-risk group.
Q: What is the difference between a term and an accidental insurance policy?
An accidental insurance policy offers a death payment if the policyholder dies as a result of an accident. A term insurance plan, on the other hand, covers death from any cause, natural or accidental.
Q: Are premiums subject to vary over time?
It relies on a number of things, including the inclusion of riders, the disclosure of habits such as smoking, drinking, or the declaration of a hazardous occupational nature, among others.
Q: What is the difference between term and endowment?
An endowment contains maturity benefits, but a term insurance policy does not. However in case of both term insurance policy and endowment the policyholder's nominee(s) are entitled to the amount guaranteed in the case of the policyholder's death within the plan period.
Q: With term insurance, are there no maturity proceeds?
Nowadays, many insurance carriers include a provision for return of premium, which allows the policyholder to collect the paid premiums in the case of plan maturity, but this leads to increase in the premiums. However a typical term plan is without any maturity benefit
Q: Is the insured person's death considered if he or she dies outside of Indian Territory?
Once in force, term insurance entitles the individual's nominee to benefits even if the person dies outside of India.
Q: Is it possible to accept numerous claims?
Multiple claims from various insurers may be accepted, provided that these claims and their specific nature were reported at the time of policy purchase.
Q: Is it possible for NRIs to get term insurance in India?
NRIs with dual citizenship who qualify as Indian nationals are entitled to get term insurance in India.
Q: Do term insurance policies cover deaths caused by "Acts of God"?
Death caused by an act of God is not excluded unless specifically stated in the insurance document's exclusions.
Q: What tax advantages are available with a basic term insurance plan?
A term insurance plan is eligible for tax exemption under Section 80C up to a limit of Rs. 1.5 Lac in any given financial year.
Q. Is it worthwhile to purchase insurance policies that are covered under the Married Women's Property Act?
Yes, but only in exceptional instances. Many insurers provide a provision in their application forms that allows you to enroll in a policy under the Married Women's Property Act (MWP Act). Term insurance purchased under this section (section 6) may be paid to a trust under a specified provision of the Act (section 6). If you die unexpectedly, the death benefit from such plans may only be received by the trust and distributed to the beneficiaries you selected in your policy (can only be your wife or children). The money collected from these plans cannot be taken by lenders with whom you may have outstanding debts, or by relatives or family members who are beneficiaries under your Will.
These insurance are handy if you have a lot of money and are worried about family feuds, or if you think you'll have a lot of claims on your money when you die, or if you have a lot of debt from operating a company. To take advantage of this benefit, you may need to include an addendum to your proposal form, precisely stating the beneficiaries and the percentage of the amount insured you want to leave to each of them.
Wrapping Up
Bajaj Allianz Life provides a variety of term insurance policies aimed at protecting your loved ones' financial futures and streamlining monetary reserves for their unique advantages.
